sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a clear research vision focused on trauma-informed care in EDs (g.1.a) and proposes a distinctive approach using arts-based co-design (g.1.b). The team includes a mix of academic and clinical expertise, suggesting some institutional support (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's career trajectory or long-term vision (g.1.a). No training plan is described (g.1.c). Mentorship arrangements are implied by roles but not explicitly detailed as a support framework (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's career trajectory or long-term vision (g.1.a). No training plan is described (g.1.c). Mentorship arrangements are implied by roles but not explicitly detailed as a support framework (g.1.d).

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: The application mentions partnerships with NHS trusts and community organizations, indicating some development of research partnerships (g.2.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application does not explain why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a), nor does it articulate the difference securing the award would make to their career trajectory (g.2.b). There is no discussion of new skills to be gained (g.2.c). The text focuses on project delivery rather than career development.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application does not explain why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a), nor does it articulate the difference securing the award would make to their career trajectory (g.2.b). There is no discussion of new skills to be gained (g.2.c). The text focuses on project delivery rather than career development.

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: The lead applicant mentions managing large NIHR studies, indicating prior project experience (g.3.a). The team composition suggests alignment between prior experience (e.g., service design, clinical oversight) and project needs (g.3.c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications relevant to the proposed study (g.3.b). The description of prior experience is brief and lacks specific details on methods or techniques used in previous projects (g.3.a).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications relevant to the proposed study (g.3.b). The description of prior experience is brief and lacks specific details on methods or techniques used in previous projects (g.3.a).

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text contains no list of publications, reports, or presentations (g.4.a). There are no citation metrics or impact indicators mentioned (g.4.b). No awards or grants linked to prior research are listed (g.4.c). There is no description of the practical or societal impact of previous work (g.4.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text contains no list of publications, reports, or presentations (g.4.a). There are no citation metrics or impact indicators mentioned (g.4.b). No awards or grants linked to prior research are listed (g.4.c). There is no description of the practical or societal impact of previous work (g.4.d).

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: The lead applicant's role includes strategic leadership and project governance, suggesting increasing responsibility (g.5.b).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There are no explicit statements of career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No participation in leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). There is no evidence of peer or institutional recognition suggesting leadership potential (g.5.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There are no explicit statements of career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No participation in leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). There is no evidence of peer or institutional recognition suggesting leadership potential (g.5.d).

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: Co-applicant Dr. Leech is from the Emergency Department and provides clinical insight, demonstrating involvement in practice-based research (g.6.b).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions (g.6.a). No evidence is provided regarding balancing practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c). No participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications is mentioned (g.6.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions (g.6.a). No evidence is provided regarding balancing practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c). No participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications is mentioned (g.6.d).

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is exceptionally clear, with NLP metrics showing a Flesch-Kincaid grade of 7.45 and 0% long sentences. It explicitly covers the problem, objectives, methods (Experience-Based Co-Design), beneficiaries, and impact. The text confirms it was rewritten with survivor-experts to eliminate complex language, ensuring high accessibility and alignment with the detailed proposal.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary perfectly meets all criteria for readability, jargon explanation, and content alignment.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary perfectly meets all criteria for readability, jargon explanation, and content alignment.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The Scientific Abstract clearly identifies a specific knowledge gap regarding the lack of evidence on introducing trauma-informed practices into Emergency Departments. It logically connects the problem (survivors avoiding care) to the proposed solution.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text is limited to the Abstract and does not contain a comprehensive literature review with specific citations or critical analysis of existing studies, which limits the score for reference quality and depth of critical analysis.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text is limited to the Abstract and does not contain a comprehensive literature review with specific citations or critical analysis of existing studies, which limits the score for reference quality and depth of critical analysis.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The project scope is well-defined with a 24-month timeline, specific phases (M1-24), and clear sample sizes (n=20 staff, n=30 survivors). Feasibility is strongly supported by detailed mitigation strategies for staff turnover and ED pressures, and specific FTE commitments are outlined.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award level and resources.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award level and resources.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described, integrating Experience-Based Co-Design with a behavior change framework. The design includes specific phases for discovery, defining functions, and prototyping. Safeguards for recruitment and site selection (2 differentiated sites) are clearly detailed, ensuring methodological rigor.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the qualitative methods are robust, the text does not explicitly detail specific control mechanisms or statistical power calculations, which is expected for a qualitative co-design study but limits the 'controls' signal slightly.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the qualitative methods are robust, the text does not explicitly detail specific control mechanisms or statistical power calculations, which is expected for a qualitative co-design study but limits the 'controls' signal slightly.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly articulates benefits for survivors (improved health, reduced distress), the NHS (efficiencies, lower costs), and policy (service design). It outlines a clear pathway from co-design to a feasibility study and potential nationwide rollout.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the impact is specific, well-aligned with health priorities, and clearly described.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the impact is specific, well-aligned with health priorities, and clearly described.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Engagement is central to the design, utilizing Experience-Based Co-Design with survivors and staff across all phases. A survivor-led organization (Survivors Voices) is embedded to lead recruitment, facilitate co-design, and provide peer support. Ethical considerations and safeguards are explicitly detailed.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the engagement plan is comprehensive and deeply integrated.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the engagement plan is comprehensive and deeply integrated.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The budget provides explicit links between tasks and resources, including detailed costs for staff time, travel, inclusion activities (interpreters, translation), and creative outputs. The rationale for funding levels is clear, with specific justifications for increased PPI time and inclusion costs.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the resource justification is thorough and proportional to the scope.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the resource justification is thorough and proportional to the scope.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The Application Context mentions the Lead Applicant has managed large NIHR studies and the team includes experienced co-applicants with specific roles (e.g., service design, clinical oversight).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text does not contain a detailed list of prior publications, specific successful projects, or a comprehensive CV section to fully assess the quality and relevance of previous research outputs. Evidence is limited to brief role descriptions.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text does not contain a detailed list of prior publications, specific successful projects, or a comprehensive CV section to fully assess the quality and relevance of previous research outputs. Evidence is limited to brief role descriptions.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The application includes a robust inclusion strategy targeting underserved communities (e.g., Black and people of colour, migrant women). It details adaptive methods (interpreters, translation, remote options) and allocates specific budget (£8,513.73) for inclusion activities, ensuring equity and accessibility.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the inclusive design is explicit, well-resourced, and covers the full research lifecycle.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the inclusive design is explicit, well-resourced, and covers the full research lifecycle.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding a specific training plan, description, or structure.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text provided (Application Context) lists team roles and FTE commitments but contains no section detailing a training plan, specific training activities, or a timeline for skill acquisition. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.1' as a missing signal.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text provided (Application Context) lists team roles and FTE commitments but contains no section detailing a training plan, specific training activities, or a timeline for skill acquisition. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.1' as a missing signal.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding personal skill gaps or targeted training activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The text describes the roles of the lead applicant and co-applicants but does not identify specific skill gaps for any individual, nor does it map training activities to project tasks or mention mentorship plans. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.2' as a missing signal.
sub_criteria.rationale: The text describes the roles of the lead applicant and co-applicants but does not identify specific skill gaps for any individual, nor does it map training activities to project tasks or mention mentorship plans. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.2' as a missing signal.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding career goals or the link between training and career trajectory.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text focuses on project governance and specific research roles rather than long-term career aspirations or how the project's activities will serve as professional development for the applicants. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.3' as a missing signal.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text focuses on project governance and specific research roles rather than long-term career aspirations or how the project's activities will serve as professional development for the applicants. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.3' as a missing signal.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly identifies the contracting organisation (University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust) and partners (Coventry University, Reshapers CIC), confirming a collaborative structure (ss.1.c). The team composition includes academic researchers, NHS clinicians, and a survivor-led community interest company, suggesting relevant expertise.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no specific evidence of the organisations' publication history, previous funding success, or notable achievements in the relevant research field (ss.1.a, ss.1.b, ss.1.d). The text lists names and roles but does not detail past track records or specific outputs.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no specific evidence of the organisations' publication history, previous funding success, or notable achievements in the relevant research field (ss.1.a, ss.1.b, ss.1.d). The text lists names and roles but does not detail past track records or specific outputs.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The Lead Applicant's role includes 'project oversight and support co-applicants and staff', indicating a supervisory support arrangement (ss.2.a). The team includes a co-applicant from the Emergency Department providing clinical insight, aligning supervisor expertise with project needs (ss.2.c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit description of mentorship plans, meeting frequencies, or structured supervision frameworks (ss.2.b, ss.2.d). The text describes roles but lacks detail on how supervision and career development support will be delivered.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit description of mentorship plans, meeting frequencies, or structured supervision frameworks (ss.2.b, ss.2.d). The text describes roles but lacks detail on how supervision and career development support will be delivered.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: No direct evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text does not describe specific facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the contracting organisation (ss.3.a). There are no links between organisational support and project success, nor evidence of tailored support for the award type (ss.3.b, ss.3.d). Alignment of organisational focus with career trajectory is not explicitly stated (ss.3.c).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text does not describe specific facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the contracting organisation (ss.3.a). There are no links between organisational support and project success, nor evidence of tailored support for the award type (ss.3.b, ss.3.d). Alignment of organisational focus with career trajectory is not explicitly stated (ss.3.c).

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: No direct evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The text contains no mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within the organisation (ss.4.a). There is no evidence of inclusive recruitment initiatives, adherence to research integrity principles, or statements on organisational culture (ss.4.b, ss.4.c, ss.4.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The text contains no mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within the organisation (ss.4.a). There is no evidence of inclusive recruitment initiatives, adherence to research integrity principles, or statements on organisational culture (ss.4.b, ss.4.c, ss.4.d).

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a robust integration of lived experience through Experience-Based Co-Design (EBCD) across all phases. A survivor-led organization (Survivors Voices) is embedded as a co-applicant to shape recruitment, intervention development, and data analysis. Specific changes to the design, such as the use of arts-based methods and a short film to stimulate co-design, are explicitly linked to PPI input.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for integration is strong, the specific examples of changes made to the *initial* study design based on prior lived experience (before this application) are less detailed than the plan for future co-design activities, though the 'Changes from Previous Stage' section does address committee feedback on this.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for integration is strong, the specific examples of changes made to the *initial* study design based on prior lived experience (before this application) are less detailed than the plan for future co-design activities, though the 'Changes from Previous Stage' section does address committee feedback on this.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The team composition is diverse in terms of expertise, including academic researchers, NHS clinicians, and a survivor-led community interest company. The inclusion of an 'Inclusion Lead' (Little Ro) and specific interviewers to reflect participant identity demonstrates a commitment to equitable collaboration and representation.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of the demographic characteristics (gender, ethnicity, age) of the research team members themselves, only their professional roles and the diversity of the participants they aim to recruit.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of the demographic characteristics (gender, ethnicity, age) of the research team members themselves, only their professional roles and the diversity of the participants they aim to recruit.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: The application outlines a clear dissemination plan involving accessible formats (short film, creative outputs) and engagement with community organizations and professional bodies. The link between the co-designed intervention and anticipated benefits (improved ED care, reduced NHS costs, better health for survivors) is explicit.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan is strong, the specific mechanisms for measuring the 'meaningful benefit' to the public beyond the feasibility study phase are not fully detailed, as the current stage focuses on intervention development rather than evaluation of outcomes.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan is strong, the specific mechanisms for measuring the 'meaningful benefit' to the public beyond the feasibility study phase are not fully detailed, as the current stage focuses on intervention development rather than evaluation of outcomes.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies seldom-heard groups (migrant women, cultural minorities, those with insecure housing) and details targeted strategies including an Inclusion team, translation into 5 languages, interpreters, and interviewers reflecting participant identity. Barriers to participation are discussed with specific mitigation strategies (e.g., peer support, flexible participation).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the evidence for representation and mitigation is comprehensive and well-resourced.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the evidence for representation and mitigation is comprehensive and well-resourced.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the entire lifecycle: design (PPI partner shaping methods), recruitment (inclusive strategies), conduct (peer support, interpreters), and dissemination (accessible creative outputs). The budget explicitly supports inclusion activities at every stage.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation are mentioned (e.g., monitoring barriers), the specific metrics or feedback loops for evaluating the *success* of the inclusion strategy itself are less detailed than the inclusion activities.
sub_criteria.rationale: While plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation are mentioned (e.g., monitoring barriers), the specific metrics or feedback loops for evaluating the *success* of the inclusion strategy itself are less detailed than the inclusion activities.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a fully costed plan for inclusion, with a dedicated budget line (£8,513.73) exceeding the usual tier limit. Specific costs are allocated for PPI partner time, interpreters, translation, travel for carers, and participant care costs, ensuring the inclusion plan is fully resourced.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the alignment between the inclusion plan and allocated resources is precise and well-justified.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the alignment between the inclusion plan and allocated resources is precise and well-justified.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application form analysis confirms the presence of 7 distinct sections with clear hierarchy (numbered_heading_like_lines=7) and 26 list markers, indicating strong structural organization. Extracted emphasis markers (43) suggest effective use of bolding or highlighting, though parser limitations mean some may be lost.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser limitation note indicates that some bold/emphasis formatting may have been lost during extraction, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b as the full extent of emphasis cannot be verified.
sub_criteria.rationale: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser limitation note indicates that some bold/emphasis formatting may have been lost during extraction, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b as the full extent of emphasis cannot be verified.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: Duplication metrics are excellent, with a duplicate sentence rate of 0.008 and repeated line rate of 0.008, indicating minimal unnecessary repetition. Lexical overlap between sections is low (0.189-0.235), suggesting distinct content across sections.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While overlap is low, the presence of any lexical overlap (up to 0.235) between the Summary Budget and Application Context suggests some inevitable repetition of financial figures or context, preventing a perfect score for novelty.
sub_criteria.rationale: While overlap is low, the presence of any lexical overlap (up to 0.235) between the Summary Budget and Application Context suggests some inevitable repetition of financial figures or context, preventing a perfect score for novelty.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates strong logical flow with 21 transition phrases and 42 terms linking objectives, methods, and budget. The section order follows a logical progression from context to abstract, research plan, PPI, and budget.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structural indicators for flow are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts and section ordering rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread, leaving a minor gap in confirming the depth of the narrative coherence.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the structural indicators for flow are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts and section ordering rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread, leaving a minor gap in confirming the depth of the narrative coherence.
